results.title: Towards school
results.creator_credit: Antonio Colacino, Malcolm Otton and Film Australia
results.brief_description:
Episode number 4 of Series “The Developing child”
Preschool is, for the child, the gateway between the familiarity of home, and the formal education system. Shows children at play, developing and practising new skills.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Antonio Colacino, Malcolm Otton and Film Australia

results.title: Pregnancy and birth
results.creator_credit: Antonio Colacino, Malcolm Otton and Film Australia
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Developing child”.
Derek Llewellyn-Jones, Professor of Obstetrics, University of Sydney, stresses the importance of prenatal care, child birth education and psychoprophylaxis in promoting relaxation and confidence during the birth process. An actual birth is shown.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Antonio Colacino, Malcolm Otton and Film Australia

results.title: Space
results.brief_description:
Episode number 3 of Series “The Dance”.
Space is the dancer’s canvas. In this film, animation is used in addition to live action to give a picture of space both inside and outside the dancer’s body.

results.title: Motion
results.creator_credit: Murray Louis and Jack Lieb Productions
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “Dance as an art form”
Part of a series designed to introduce dance as an art form. Illustrates how commonplace actions can be sensed and then transformed through selection, arrangement and skillful performance. Narrated by Murray Louis.

results.title: Time
results.brief_description:
Episode number 4 of Series “The Dance series”
For the dancer, time is the awareness of duration. The film moves from the traditional musical definition of time into other areas of the time sense.

results.title: Shape
results.creator_credit: Murray Louis and American Federation of Arts
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Dance”.
The sculptural dynamics of the dancer’s body is the focus of this film. The dancer is revealed as a sculptor who moulds and transforms himself into forms that lend their force to the eloquence of dance.

group_works.title: Who killed the sale?
group_works.creator_credit: Michael Tuchner, Anthony Pelissier, Ian Latimer and Rank Organisation Short Films Group

results.title: Who killed the sale?
results.creator_credit: Michael Tuchner, Anthony Pelissier, Ian Latimer and Rank Organisation Short Films Group
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Customer and You”.
An amusing film in Cleese style of a case study which shows that employees in all parts of a company can affect a company’s ability to get and to keep business. With Bud Tingwell.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Michael Tuchner, Anthony Pelissier, Ian Latimer and Rank Organisation Short Films Group
